explanation: The PAC Chairperson is appointed by the Speaker (by convention from the Opposition), not "elected by the members" — so statement c is false. The PAC has 15 Lok Sabha + 7 Rajya Sabha members, examines accounts/CAG reports, and the Speaker's role in overlapping memberships are correct.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following committees does not have members from the Rajya Sabha?
options: The Committee on the Welfare of Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes
explanation: The Estimates Committee is composed entirely of 30 Lok Sabha members and has no Rajya Sabha members. The PAC, the Committee on Public Undertakings and the SC/ST Welfare Committee all include Rajya Sabha members.

subject: Polity
stem: A Joint Sitting of the Parliament is resorted to, for resolving the deadlock between two Houses of the Parliament for passing which of the following Bills?
subs: 1. Money Bill
2. Constitutional Amendment Bill
3. Ordinary Bill
Select the correct answer using the code given below
explanation: A joint sitting (Article 108) is available only for an Ordinary Bill deadlock. Money Bills don't need it (the Lok Sabha prevails anyway), and Constitutional Amendment Bills require each House to pass them separately by special majority — no joint sitting. Hence 3 only.

subject: Polity
stem: Which of the following statements regarding the casting vote in the Parliament is/are correct?
subs: 1. It is cast by the speaker or a person acting as such.
2. It is cast in addition to voting in the first instance.
3. It is cast in the case of equality of votes.
4. It is always cast to maintain the status quo.
Select the correct answer using the code given below
explanation: The casting vote is exercised by the presiding officer (Speaker) (1), and only in case of a tie (3). Statement 2 is wrong — the Speaker does not vote in the first instance (he votes only to break a tie), and statement 4 ("always" status quo) is too absolute. Hence 1 and 3 only.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one among the following motions cannot be made while introducing an ordinary Bill in the Parliament?
options: That the Bill be taken into consideration
That the Bill be circulated for the purpose of eliciting public opinion
That the Bill be referred to a Select Committee
That the Bill be referred to a Joint Committee of the House without the concurrence of the other House
explanation: A bill can be referred to a Joint Committee only with the other House's concurrence — so a motion to refer it to a joint committee without that concurrence is not permissible (d). The other three motions (consideration, circulation, Select Committee) are valid.

subject: Polity
stem: A motion of no-con dence is moved against
explanation: A no-confidence motion (in the Lok Sabha) is directed against the entire Council of Ministers (the government as a whole, which is collectively responsible), not a single minister, the PM personally, or a party.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following statements about the Speaker of Lok Sabha is not correct?
options: He shall vacate his office if he ceases to be a member of the House of the People.
He may, at any time, resign by writing his resignation to the President of India.
He may be removed from his office by a resolution of the House of the People passed by majority of all the then members of the House.
While the office of the Speaker is vacant, the duties of the office shall be performed by the Deputy Speaker.
explanation: The Speaker resigns by writing to the Deputy Speaker (not the President) — so statement b is false. Vacating office on ceasing to be a member, removal by an effective majority resolution, and the Deputy Speaker filling a vacancy are correct.

subject: Polity
stem: Consider the following:
subs: 1. M.A. Ayyangar
2. G.S. Dhillon
3. Balram Jakhar
4. P.A. Sangma How many of them were the Speaker of the Lok Sabha?
explanation: All four — M. A. Ayyangar, G. S. Dhillon, Balram Jakhar and P. A. Sangma — served as Speaker of the Lok Sabha at different times. Hence 4.

subject: Polity
stem: Match List-I with List-II and select the correct answer
subs: List-I (Speaker of Lok
List-II (Tenure) Sabha)
1. 14th Lok
A. Manohar Gajanan Joshi Sabha
2. 13th Lok
B. Somnath Chatterjee Sabha
3. 16th Lok
C. Meira Kumar Sabha
4. 15th Lok
D. Sumitra Mahajan Sabha
Code: A B C D
explanation: Manohar Joshi → 13th Lok Sabha (2); Somnath Chatterjee → 14th (1); Meira Kumar → 15th (4); Sumitra Mahajan → 16th (3). So A-2, B-1, C-4, D-3.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one among the following statements about an adjournment motion in the Parliament of India is not correct?
options: Its objective is to draw attention of the House to any recent matter of urgent public importance.
Adjournment motion is an extraordinary procedure.
This motion can be invoked on any matter of urgent importance that may even be within the jurisdiction of a State Government.
The Chair has absolute discretion to refuse consent.
explanation: An adjournment motion can be raised only on a matter of urgent public importance involving the Union government — not a State subject — so statement c is false. Its purpose, its extraordinary nature (it involves an element of censure), and the Chair's discretion are correct.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following statements about Motions in the Parliament of India is correct?
options: A Motion of No-Con dence can be admissible against an individual minister in the Council of Ministers.
A No-Con dence Motion must set out grounds on which it is based.
Rajya Sabha is not empowered to entertain a motion of No-Con dence.
A Censure Motion need not be based on specific grounds or charges.
explanation: A no-confidence motion can be moved only in the Lok Sabha (since the government is responsible to it) — so statement c is correct. The others are wrong: no-confidence is against the whole Council (not an individual minister) and need not state grounds, while a Censure Motion must state specific charges.

subject: Polity
stem: A person is disqualified for being chosen as, and for being, a Member of either House of the Parliament if the person
subs: 1. holds any office of pro t under the Government of India or the Government of any State other than an office declared by the Parliament by law not to disqualify its holder
2. is an undischarged insolvent
3. is so disqualified under the Tenth Schedule of the Constitution of India
4. is of unsound mind and stands so declared by a competent Court
Select the correct answer using the code given below
explanation: All four are grounds of disqualification — holding an office of profit (1), being an undischarged insolvent (2), defection under the Tenth Schedule (3), and being of unsound mind (4) (along with non-citizenship). Hence all four.

subject: Polity
stem: Which of the following statements is not correct regarding the Members of Parliament Local Area Development Scheme (MPLADS)?
options: Members of the Parliament (MPs) sanction, execute and complete works under the scheme.
Nominated Members of the Parliament can recommend works for implementation anywhere in the country.
The scheme is fully funded by the Government of India.
The annual entitlement per MP is s5 crore.
explanation: Under MPLADS, MPs only recommend works — the district authority sanctions and executes them — so statement a is false. The scheme being fully GoI-funded, nominated MPs recommending works anywhere, and the ₹5 crore annual entitlement are correct.

subject: Polity
stem: Which of the following statements with regard to the privileges of the Members of the Parliament are correct?
subs: 1. Privileges would not be fettered by the Article 191a of the Constitution of India.
2. Privileges must be read subject to the Articles 20-22 and Article 32 of the Constitution of India.
3. Immunity is available in relation to both civil and criminal prosecution.
4. Immunity is available in relation to freedom of speech even in his/her private or personal capacity.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
explanation: Parliamentary privileges (freedom of speech in the House under Article 105) are not limited by the ordinary free-speech restrictions (1) but are read subject to fundamental safeguards like Articles 20-22 and 32 (2). Statements 3 and 4 are too broad — the immunity covers only things said/votes given in the House, not general civil/criminal acts or private speech. Hence 1 and 2 only — verify the official key.

subject: Polity
stem: Which among the following is not a condition for the disqualification of a Member of Parliament?
options: Voluntary acquisition of citizenship of a foreign country
Holding the office of the Chairperson of the National Commission for Women
The Member abstains from voting in the House without prior permission
The Member holds the office of the Chairman of the Board of Directors of the National Coal Development Corporation Ltd.
explanation: The office of Chairperson of the National Commission for Women is exempted from disqualification under the Parliament (Prevention of Disqualification) Act, so holding it does not disqualify an MP. Abstaining from voting, or voting against the party whip, can attract Tenth Schedule (anti-defection) consequences depending on prior permission or condonation.

subject: Polity
stem: Article 191 of the Constitution of India, as it stands amended, includes which of the following?
subs: 1. Freedom of speech and expression
2. Assemble peaceably and without arms
3. To acquire and dispose property
4. To move freely throughout the territory of India
Select the correct answer using the code given below
explanation: Article 19 (originally with seven freedoms) now has six, after the right to property [19(1)(f)] was removed by the 44th Amendment. So speech (1), assembly (2) and free movement (4) remain, but acquiring/disposing of property (3) is no longer in Article 19. Hence 1, 2 and 4 only.

subject: Polity
stem: Any question pertaining to the disqualification of a member of the Lok Sabha on the ground of defection is decided by
options: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha
explanation: Under the Tenth Schedule (Anti-Defection Law), disqualification on the ground of defection is decided by the presiding officer — the Speaker of the Lok Sabha (subject to judicial review). The President decides only other disqualifications, on the EC's opinion.

subject: Polity
stem: Which of the following descriptions with regard to Parliamentary Privilege for Members of the Parliament of India is/are correct?
subs: 1. The Speaker is empowered to refer suo moto any question of privilege for investigation
2. A question of privilege is accorded priority over other items in the List of Business
3. The privilege extends to freedom from preventive arrest or detention
Select the answer using the code given below
explanation: The Speaker may refer a question of privilege to the Committee of Privileges (1), and such questions take priority in the day's business (2). Statement 3 is wrong — an MP's freedom from arrest is only in civil cases; it does not cover criminal matters or preventive detention. Hence 1 and 2 only.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following statements about the composition of the Parliament is not correct?
options: Representatives of the states in Rajya Sabha are elected directly by the people.
Representatives from Union Territories in Lok Sabha are chosen by direct elections.
Lok Sabha has seats reserved for SCs and STs.
explanation: Rajya Sabha members representing the states are indirectly elected — by the elected members of the State Legislative Assemblies (by STV) — not directly by the people. So statement a is false. The direct election of UT Lok Sabha members, 12 nominated Rajya Sabha members, and SC/ST reservation in the Lok Sabha are correct.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one among the following correctly represents the theme on which the interiors of the new Parliament Building is based? Lok Sabha - Rajya Sabha
explanation: In the new Parliament building, the Lok Sabha chamber carries the peacock (national bird) theme and the Rajya Sabha the lotus (national flower) theme. (The Central Lounge has a banyan-tree theme.)

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following is not true about the powers of the Supreme Court?
options: The Supreme Court has original and exclusive jurisdiction in inter-governmental disputes.
The Supreme Court has advisory jurisdiction on a question of law or fact which may be referred to it by the President of India.
The Supreme Court has the power to review its own judgment or order.
The Supreme Court has the exclusive power to issue writs to protect the fundamental rights of the people.
explanation: The power to issue writs for fundamental rights is not exclusive to the Supreme Court — the High Courts also issue writs under Article 226 (with even wider scope). So statement d is false. The SC's original jurisdiction in centre-state disputes, advisory jurisdiction (Article 143) and review power are correct.

subject: Polity
stem: Which of the following are the powers of the Supreme Court of India?
subs: 1. Original jurisdiction in a dispute between the Government of India and one or more States
2. The power to hear appeals from the High Courts
3. Passing decrees and orders for doing justice in any matter before it
4. Render advice to the President of India in matters of law
Select the correct answer using the code given below
explanation: The Supreme Court has original jurisdiction in centre-state disputes (1, Article 131), appellate jurisdiction over High Courts (2), the power to do "complete justice" (3, Article 142), and advisory jurisdiction (4, Article 143). All four.

subject: Polity
stem: The power of the Supreme Court to decide in the case of a dispute between two or more States is called
explanation: Disputes between states, or between the Centre and states, fall under the Supreme Court's original jurisdiction (Article 131) — meaning they go straight to the Supreme Court, not on appeal.

subject: Polity
stem: The landmark Supreme Court judgment in the case Subhash Sharma v. Union of India 1991 refers to which one of the following basic features of the Constitution of India?
explanation: The Subhash Sharma case (1991) dealt with judicial appointments and the independence/role of the judiciary, and was referred to a larger bench, leading to the Second Judges case — it is associated with judicial review/independence of the judiciary among the listed basic features. (The mapping is imperfect; verify with the official key.)

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following criteria is not required to be qualified for appointment as Judge of the Supreme Court?
options: At leastfive years as a Judge of a High Court
At least ten years as an Advocate of a High Court
In the opinion of the President, a distinguished Jurist
At least twenty years as a Sub-Judicial Magistrate
explanation: The qualifications for a Supreme Court judge are: 5 years as a High Court judge, or 10 years as a High Court advocate, or being a distinguished jurist. "Twenty years as a Sub-Judicial Magistrate" (d) is not a qualifying criterion.

subject: Polity
stem: Article 231 of the Constitution of India grants power to establish a common High Court for two or more states to
explanation: Under Article 231, Parliament may by law establish a common High Court for two or more States or for a State and a UT (e.g. the Punjab & Haryana High Court). Not the Supreme Court, President or Cabinet.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following statements is not correct?
options: Allahabad High Court has a Bench at Lucknow.
Madhya Pradesh High Court has a Bench at Gwalior.
Rajasthan High Court is located at Jodhpur.
Guwahati High Court has no Bench.
explanation: The Gauhati (Guwahati) High Court does have benches (e.g. at Kohima, Aizawl, Itanagar serving the north-eastern states) — so statement d is false. The Allahabad HC's Lucknow bench, the MP HC's Gwalior bench, and the Rajasthan HC at Jodhpur are correct.

subject: Polity
stem: The basic structure doctrine with regard to the Constitution of India relates to
subs: 1. the power of judicial review
2. the judgment in Kesavananda Bharati case 1973
3. the constraints on Article 368 of the Constitution of India
4. the judgment in case 1967 Golaknath
Select the correct answer using the code given below
explanation: The basic structure doctrine — that Parliament cannot destroy the Constitution's essential framework — was laid down in Kesavananda Bharati (1973) (2), is a limit on the amending power under Article 368 (3), and is enforced through judicial review (1). The 1967 Golaknath case (4) is a different, earlier ruling and did not give the doctrine (Kesavananda overruled it). Hence 1, 2 and 3 only.

subject: Polity
stem: Who among the following was the Chief Justice of India when Public Interest Litigation (PIL) was introduced in the Indian Judicial System?
explanation: Justice P. N. Bhagwati (with Justice Krishna Iyer) pioneered Public Interest Litigation in India, relaxing locus standi so that anyone could approach the court on behalf of the disadvantaged.

subject: Polity
stem: The judgement of the Supreme Court of India in the Vishakha Case pertains to
explanation: Vishakha v. State of Rajasthan (1997) laid down binding guidelines against the sexual harassment of women at the workplace (later codified in the 2013 Act). It is not about sati, dowry death or rape as such.

subject: Polity
stem: Which among the following statements about the power to change the basic structure of the Constitution of India is/are correct?
subs: 1. It falls outside the scope of the powers of the Parliament.
2. It can be exercised by the people through representatives in Constituent Assembly.
3. It falls within the constituent powers of the Parliament.
Select the correct answer using the code given below
explanation: Altering the basic structure is beyond Parliament's amending/constituent power (1) — only the people, through a fresh Constituent Assembly, could do it (2). Statement 3 contradicts the doctrine, so it is wrong. Hence 1 and 2.

subject: Polity
stem: The issue, whether Section 377 of the Indian Penal Code is violative of Articles 21, 14 and 15 of the Constitution of India was decided in which one of the following cases?
options: Shreya Singhal vs. Union of India
Shayara Bano vs. Union of India
Naz Foundation vs. Government of NCT of Delhi and others
explanation: The Naz Foundation case (Delhi High Court, 2009) first read down Section 377 as violating Articles 21, 14 and 15 (the Supreme Court finally decriminalised it in Navtej Singh Johar, 2018). Shreya Singhal was on Section 66A (IT Act); Shayara Bano on triple talaq.

subject: Polity
stem: Who among the following led the Writ Petition that led to the famous verdict of the Supreme Court of India recognising the Right to Privacy as a Fundamental Right?
explanation: The 2017 verdict declaring the Right to Privacy a Fundamental Right (under Article 21) came in Justice K. S. Puttaswamy (Retd.) v. Union of India — the petition was led by retired Justice K. S. Puttaswamy.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following organizations challenged the constitutionality of Section 377 of the Indian Penal Code in the Supreme Court of India?
explanation: The Naz Foundation (India) Trust, an NGO working on HIV/AIDS and sexual-health rights, filed the petition challenging Section 377. Bachpan Bachao Andolan works against child labour; the others are different NGOs.

subject: Polity
stem: Which of the following statements about the Ordinance-making power of the Governor is/ are correct?
subs: 1. It is a discretionary power.
2. The Governor himself is not competent to withdraw the Ordinance at any time.
Select the correct answer using the code given below
explanation: Both are wrong — the Governor issues an ordinance (Article 213) on the aid and advice of the Council of Ministers (so it is not his discretionary power), and the Governor can withdraw an ordinance at any time. Hence neither.

subject: Polity
stem: Which of the following statements relating to the duties of the Governor is/are correct?
subs: 1. The duties of the Governor as a constitutional Head of the State do not become the subject matter of questions or debate in the Parliament.

2. Where the Governor takes a decision independently of his Council of Ministers or where he acts as the Chief Executive of the State under President's rule, his actions are subject to scrutiny by the Parliament.
Select the correct answer using the code given below
explanation: As a constitutional head acting on ministerial advice, the Governor's conduct is shielded from parliamentary debate (1); but when he acts in his discretion or runs the State under President's Rule (as the Centre's agent), those actions can be scrutinised by Parliament (2). Both correct.

subject: Polity
stem: Who has the power of annulment or suspension of Acts and Resolutions of the Autonomous District and Regional Councils?
options: The Chief Minister of the State
explanation: Under the Sixth Schedule, the Governor can annul or suspend any act or resolution of the Autonomous District/Regional Councils if it endangers public safety, etc.

subject: Polity
stem: Which of the following is not related to the powers of the Governor?
options: Summoning, proroguing and dissolving State Legislature
Power to grant pardons, reprieves, respites or punishments remission of
explanation: A Governor has no diplomatic or military powers (those belong to the President/Union) — so option a is not a gubernatorial power. Appointing the Advocate General, summoning/proroguing/dissolving the legislature, and granting pardons (Article 161) are all Governor's powers.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following regarding the ordinance-making power of the Governor is not correct?
options: It is not a discretionary power.
The Governor may withdraw the ordinance anytime.
The ordinance power can be exercised when the Legislature is not in session.
The aid and advice of ministers is not required for declaring the ordinance.
explanation: The Governor issues an ordinance on the aid and advice of the Council of Ministers — so saying "aid and advice is not required" is false. It being non-discretionary, withdrawable at any time, and usable only when the legislature is not in session are all correct.

subject: Polity
stem: A special address by the Governor refers to the address delivered by the Governor
options: when President's Rule is called for
when a national emergency necessitates dissolution of Legislative Assembly
at the commencement of the first session after general election and at the first session of each year
whenever he/she has concluded that such is necessary
explanation: Under Article 176, the Governor addresses the State Legislature (a "special address") at the first session after each general election and at the first session of every year, outlining the government's policy. The other options are incorrect.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following is not among the duties of the Chief Minister?
options: To communicate to the Governor of the State all decisions of the Council of Ministers relating to the administration of the affairs of the State and proposals for legislation
To furnish information relating to the administration of the State and proposals for legislation as the Governor may call for
To communicate to the President all decisions of the Council of Ministers relating to the administration of the State in the monthly report
To submit for the consideration of the Council of Ministers any matter on which a decision has been taken by a Minister but has not been considered by the Council, if the Governor so requires
explanation: The Chief Minister's duties (Article 167) are towards the Governor, not the President — so "communicate to the President" is wrong. Communicating Cabinet decisions to the Governor, furnishing information he asks for, and placing un-considered matters before the Council are the genuine duties.

subject: Polity
stem: Who among the following was India'sfirst Woman Chief Minister?
explanation: Sucheta Kripalani became India's first woman Chief Minister (Uttar Pradesh, 1963). Sarojini Naidu was the first woman Governor; Vijaya Lakshmi Pandit a diplomat/UNGA President.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one among the following States of India has the largest number of seats in its Legislative Assembly?
explanation: Among the options, West Bengal has the biggest Assembly (294 seats); Tamil Nadu has 234, Bihar 243, Madhya Pradesh 230. (Uttar Pradesh, with 403, is the largest overall but is not listed.)

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following regarding the procedure and conduct of business in the Parliament is not correct?
options: To discuss issues of the use of police force in suppressing the Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe communities
To discuss issues in dealing with violent disturbances in an Undertaking under the control of the Union Government
To discuss issues for putting down the demands of the industrial labour
explanation: Answer retained as a) To discuss State matters. Note: the uploaded question wording is unclear/garbled (source-file issue), so treat with care rather than as a clean conceptual question.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following is not considered a part of the Legislature of States?
explanation: A State Legislature consists of the Governor + the Legislative Assembly + (where it exists) the Legislative Council. The Chief Minister is part of the executive, not the legislature.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following is not a correct statement regarding the provision of Legislative Council in the State Legislature?
options: The States of Bihar and Telangana have Legislative Councils.
The total number of members in the Legislative Council of a State shall not exceed one-third of the total number of members in the Legislative Assembly.
One-twelfth of all members shall be elected by electorates consisting of local bodies and authorities.
One-twelfth of all members shall be elected by graduates residing in the State.
explanation: About one-third (not one-twelfth) of LC members are elected by local bodies — so statement c understates it. The LC total being ≤ 1/3 of the Assembly, the existence of LCs in Bihar/Telangana, and the one-twelfth graduates' quota are correct. (The split: 1/3 local bodies, 1/12 graduates, 1/12 teachers, 1/3 MLAs, 1/6 nominated.)

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following States does not have a Legislative Council?
explanation: Arunachal Pradesh has never had a Legislative Council. Karnataka and Telangana have LCs (J&K had one until it was abolished in 2019). Hence Arunachal Pradesh.

subject: Polity
stem: Which of the following States has/have bicameral legislature?
subs: 1. Andhra Pradesh
2. Telangana
3. Bihar
4. Uttar Pradesh
Select the correct answer using the code given below
explanation: All four — Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, Bihar and Uttar Pradesh — have a Legislative Council in addition to the Assembly (i.e. are bicameral). Hence all four.

subject: Polity
stem: Which of the following statements regarding Indian federal system is/are correct?
subs: 1. All States have equal representation in the Rajya Sabha.
2. Consent of a State is not required for altering its boundaries.
3. There is no dual citizenship in India.
Select the correct answer using the code given below
explanation: A State's consent is not needed to alter its boundaries (Article 3) (2), and India has single citizenship (3). Statement 1 is wrong — Rajya Sabha representation is by population, so states do not have equal seats (unlike the US Senate). Hence 2 and 3 only.

subject: Polity
stem: Consider the following statements with regard to the formation of new States and alteration of boundaries of existing States
subs: 1. Parliament may increase the area of any State.
2. Parliament may diminish the area of any State.
3. Parliament cannot alter the boundary of any State.
4. Parliament cannot alter the name of any State. Which of the statements given above is/are not correct?
explanation: Under Article 3, Parliament can increase/diminish a State's area and can alter its boundary and name. So the statements that are not correct are 3 (cannot alter boundary) and 4 (cannot alter name) — both are false. Hence 3 and 4.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following forms of Constitution contains the features of both the Unitary and Federal Constitution?
explanation: A constitution mixing federal and unitary features is termed quasi-federal — the Indian Constitution is the classic example ("federal in normal times, unitary in emergencies").

subject: Polity
stem: M.M. Punchhi Commission on Centre-State Relations has recommended the disposal of a bill reserved for the consideration of the Union Executive within
explanation: The Punchhi Commission recommended a time limit of six months for the President to decide on a bill reserved by a Governor — to prevent indefinite delay.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following is not correct?
options: Taxes on agricultural income is a subject under the State List.
Price control is a subject under the Concurrent List.
Insurance does not come under the Union List.
Forests is a subject under the Concurrent List.
explanation: Insurance IS in the Union List — so saying it does not is false. Taxes on agricultural income (State List), price control (Concurrent List) and forests (moved to the Concurrent List by the 42nd Amendment) are correctly stated.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following statements is not correct about the nature of India's federal system?
options: There is no equality of representation of States in the Council of States.
Sikkim was not initially included in the Union as a full-fledged State.
Special provisions have been laid down in the Constitution of India for Andhra Pradesh under Article 371 D.
The Constitution of India enables the federal government to acquire the strength of a unitary system during emergencies.
explanation: [Defective framing] As printed there is no valid 'not correct' option. Statement a is factually true (Rajya Sabha representation of States is unequal and population-linked), and the other statements are also factually correct - so no option is actually 'not correct'.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following is not a characteristic feature of Indian Federalism?
options: The federating units consented to form a union
Residuary powers vest with the Centre
An extensive Union and Concurrent list
explanation: Unlike the USA (formed by states "coming together"), the Indian states did not voluntarily agree to form the union — India is "an indestructible Union of destructible states" (holding together). So statement a is not a feature. Residuary powers with the Centre, single citizenship and a strong Union/Concurrent list are genuine Indian features.

subject: Polity
stem: As per the State List under the Seventh Schedule of the Constitution of India, the States have jurisdiction over agricultural land in connection with which of the following subjects?
subs: 1. Taxes on agricultural income
2. Duties in respect of succession of agricultural land
3. Estate duty in respect of agricultural land
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
explanation: All three — taxes on agricultural income, succession duty on agricultural land, and estate duty on agricultural land — are State-List subjects (agriculture being primarily a state matter). Hence 1, 2 and 3.

subject: Polity
stem: Which of the following statements is/are correct?
subs: 1. Criminal law is exclusively a subject under the State List of the Constitution of India.
2. Code of Civil Procedure falls under the Concurrent List of the Constitution of India.
Select the answer using the code given below
explanation: The Code of Civil Procedure (and Criminal Procedure, contracts, etc.) are in the Concurrent List (2, correct). Statement 1 is wrong — criminal law is in the Concurrent List, not exclusively the State List. Hence 2 only.

subject: Polity
stem: Which of the following criteria are specified for granting Special Category Status to States in India?
subs: 1. Hilly and difficult terrain
2. Economic and infrastructural backwardness
3. Strategic coastal zone
4. Sizeable share of tribal population
Select the answer using the code given below
explanation: Special Category Status (Gadgil formula) considers hilly/difficult terrain (1), economic and infrastructural backwardness (2) and a sizeable tribal population (4), plus low density and a strategic border (not coastal) location. So statement 3 is wrong; hence 1, 2 and 4 only.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following statements in respect of the States of India is not correct?
options: States in India cannot have their own Constitutions.
The State of Jammu and Kashmir has its own Constitution.
States in India do not have the right to secede from the Union of India.
The maximum number of members in the Council of Ministers of Delhi can be 15 percent of the total number of members in the Legislative Assembly.
explanation: For Delhi, the special provision caps the Council of Ministers at 10 percent of the Assembly's strength, not 15 percent — so statement d is false. States generally cannot have their own constitutions (J&K being the historic exception), and cannot secede — those are correct.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following Articles of the Constitution of India deals with the special provision with respect to the State of Assam?
explanation: Article 371B contains the special provision for Assam (regarding a committee of the Assembly for the tribal areas). 371A is for Nagaland, 371C for Manipur, 371D for Andhra Pradesh.

subject: Polity
stem: Provisions of which one of the following Articles of the Constitution of India apply to the State of Jammu and Kashmir?
explanation: Article 370 gave Jammu & Kashmir its special (temporary) autonomous status (abrogated in August 2019). Article 371G is for Mizoram; Article 238 was repealed long ago.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following regarding the tenure of the elected members of the Autonomous District Council is correct?
options: Five years from the date of election
Five years from the date appointed for the first meeting of the Council after the election
Six years from the date of administration of Oath
Six years from the date of election
explanation: Under the Sixth Schedule, elected members of the Autonomous District Council hold office for five years from the date appointed for the Council's first meeting after the election (not from the election date itself).

subject: Polity
stem: Who among the following shall cause the accounts of the Autonomous District and Regional Council Funds to be audited?
options: The Comptroller and Auditor General of India
The Chartered Accountant empanelled by the Government of India
explanation: Under the Sixth Schedule, the accounts of the District/Regional Council Funds are audited in such manner as the CAG may prescribe, and the reports are submitted to the Governor.

subject: Polity
stem: Article 371 A of the Constitution of India provides special privileges to
explanation: Article 371A gives Nagaland special protection — no Act of Parliament on Naga religious/social practices, customary law, land and resources applies unless the State Assembly so decides. Mizoram is covered by 371G, Sikkim by 371F, Manipur by 371C.

subject: Polity
stem: As per Article 371G of the Constitution of India, special provisions have been made with respect to the state of Mizoram. Accordingly, no Act of Parliament shall apply on certain matters unless the Legislative Assembly of Mizoram so decides by a resolution. Which one of the following matters is not covered under this Article?
options: Religious or social practices of the Mizos
Administration of civil and criminal justice involving decisions according to Mizo customary law
Ownership and transfer of natural resources
explanation: Article 371G protects Mizo religious/social practices, customary law, administration of justice by customary law, and ownership/transfer of land — but it speaks of land, not "natural resources" in general. So option d is not the matter covered.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following sources tells us about women protesting against the infidelity of their husbands or the neglect of the wife and children by the male head of the household?
options: Documents belonging to the Village Panchayats of Rajasthan, Gujarat and Maharashtra
explanation: (A history question filed in the Panchayat section.) Records of these village panchayats preserve women's complaints against neglect/infidelity — a rare glimpse of ordinary women's grievances. The other sources do not provide this.

subject: Polity
stem: To be eligible to contest election under the Haryana Panchayati Raj Amendment Act, 2015, a candidate should
subs: 1. have a functional toilet at home
2. have payment slips of power bills
3. not be a cooperative loan defaulter
4. have studied minimum matriculation irrespective of category
Select the correct answer using the code given below
explanation: The Haryana law required a functional toilet (1), no pending power dues (2) and no cooperative-loan default (3). Statement 4 is wrong — the minimum education requirement varied by category (lower for women/SCs), not "irrespective of category." Hence 1, 2 and 3 only.

subject: Polity
stem: Which of the following statements with regard to Panchayats in India are correct?
subs: 1. Seats in a Panchayat arefilled by direct election from the territorial constituencies in the Panchayat area.
2. The Gram Sabha is the body of persons registered in the electoral rolls relating to a village within the Panchayat area.
3. The Panchayats work on the principle of constitutional autonomy.
4. The State Legislature may by law endow the Panchayats with the power and authority to enable them to function.
Select the correct answer using the code given below
explanation: Panchayat seats are filled by direct election (1), the Gram Sabha = village electoral-roll members (2), and the State Legislature endows Panchayats with powers/authority (4). Statement 3 is wrong — Panchayats function under powers devolved by the state, not "constitutional autonomy." Hence 1, 2 and 4.

subject: Polity
stem: In which two Indian States of the four mentioned below, it is necessary to hold certain minimum educational quali cations to be eligible to contest Panchayat Elections?
subs: 1. Punjab
2. Haryana
3. Karnataka
4. Rajasthan
Select the correct answer using the code given below
explanation: Haryana (2) and Rajasthan (4) introduced minimum educational qualifications for Panchayat candidates (upheld by the Supreme Court in 2015). Punjab and Karnataka did not.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following is not correct about the Panchayats as laid down in Part IX of the Constitution of India?
options: The Chairperson of a Panchayat needs to be directly elected by people in order to exercise the right to vote in the Panchayat meetings.
The State Legislature has the right to decide whether or not of ces of the Chairpersons in the Panchayats are reserved for SCs, STs or women.
Unless dissolved earlier, every Panchayat continues for a period offive years.
The State Legislature may by law make provisions for audit of accounts of the Panchayats.
explanation: Chairpersons of the intermediate and district Panchayats are indirectly elected (by the elected members), and the mode is set by state law — so requiring direct election to vote in meetings is wrong (a). The State Legislature deciding reservations, the five-year term, and provision for audit are correct.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following statements with regard to the functioning of the Panchayats is not correct?
options: Panchayats may levy, collect and appropriate taxes, duties, tolls, etc.
A person who has attained the age of 25 years will be eligible to be a member of a Panchayat.
Every Panchayat shall ordinarily continue forfive years from the date of itsfirst meeting.
A Panchayat reconstituted after premature dissolution shall continue only for the remainder of the full period.
explanation: The minimum age to be a member of a Panchayat is 21 years, not 25 — so statement b is false. The power to levy taxes, the five-year term, and the rule that a reconstituted Panchayat serves only the remainder of the term are correct.

subject: Polity
stem: The power to legislate on all matters relating to elections to Panchayats lies with
explanation: Under Article 243K and Part IX, the State Legislatures make laws on all matters relating to Panchayat elections; the State Election Commission conducts them (superintendence/direction/control).

subject: Polity
stem: The provisions of the Constitution of India pertaining to the institution of Panchayat do not apply to which one of the following States?
explanation: Part IX (Panchayats) does not apply to the Sixth-Schedule tribal areas; Meghalaya (which is largely under the Sixth Schedule, with its own traditional councils) is exempted. Tripura, Assam (and Goa) do have Panchayats.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following statements in relation to Panchayats is not correct?
options: Legislature of a State may, by law, make provisions with respect to the composition of Panchayats.
Panchayat area means the territorial area of a Panchayat.
Gram Sabha includes all persons in the electoral rolls of village within a Panchayat.
Reservation of seats for SCs and STs has nothing to do with proportion of their population.
explanation: Reservation of seats for SCs/STs in Panchayats is proportional to their population in the area — so saying it "has nothing to do with" population is false. The State Legislature's role in composition, the meaning of "Panchayat area," and the Gram Sabha being all village voters are correct.

subject: Polity
stem: Which of the following statements about the Panchayati Raj System are correct?
subs: 1. All seats in a Panchayat arefilled by persons chosen by direct election.
2. The Gram Sabha consists of persons registered in the village electoral rolls.
3. The Chairperson of a Panchayat is elected in accordance with a law passed by each state.
4. All states in India have a three-tier system of Panchayats.
Select the correct answer using the code given below
explanation: Panchayat seats are filled by direct election (1), the Gram Sabha = village electoral-roll members (2), and the Chairperson's election follows state law (3). Statement 4 is wrong — States with a population below 20 lakh may skip the intermediate tier, so not all have three tiers. Hence 1, 2 and 3 only.

subject: Polity
stem: Which of the following statements is/are correct?
subs: 1. The elections to the Panchayat are conducted by the State Election Commission.
2. The State Election Commissioner is appointed by the Chief Election Commissioner of India.
3. The State Legislatures have the power to make laws on all matters relating to Panchayat elections.
Select the correct answer using the code given below
explanation: Panchayat elections are run by the State Election Commission (1), and the State Legislatures legislate on Panchayat-election matters (3). Statement 2 is wrong — the State Election Commissioner is appointed by the Governor, not the CEC of India. Hence 1 and 3 only.

subject: Polity
stem: Which of the following statements about Panchayats in India, as per the Constitution of India is/are correct?
subs: 1. There is a provision for reservation of seats for the Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes.
2. Panchayats are not authorised to collect taxes.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
explanation: Seats are reserved for SCs and STs in Panchayats (1, correct). Statement 2 is wrong — Panchayats are authorised (by the State Legislature) to levy and collect taxes, duties, tolls and fees. Hence 1 only.

subject: Polity
stem: Which of the following statements about the 73rd and 74th Constitution Amendment Act is/ are correct?
subs: 1. It makes it mandatory for all States to establish a three-tier system of Government.
2. Representatives should be directly elected forfive years.
3. There should be mandatory reservation of one-third of all seats in all Panchayats at all levels for women.
Select the correct answer using the code given below
explanation: The Amendments require direct election for five-year terms (2) and one-third reservation for women (3). Statement 1 is wrong — a three-tier system is not mandatory for small States (population below 20 lakh may have only two tiers). Hence 2 and 3 only.

subject: Polity
stem: The 11th Schedule of the Constitution of India distributes powers between
options: the Union and the State Legislatures
the State Legislatures and the Panchayat
the Municipal Corporation and the Panchayat
the Gram Sabha and the Panchayat
explanation: The Eleventh Schedule lists 29 subjects (agriculture, health, education, etc.) that the State Legislatures may devolve to the Panchayats under Article 243G. (The Twelfth Schedule does the same for municipalities.)

subject: Polity
stem: Which of the following statements with regard to the conduct of elections in India is/are not correct?
subs: 1. The responsibility for the preparation of the electoral rolls is vested in the Election Commission of India.
2. The Model Code of Conduct comes into existence as soon as the date of the election is announced.
3. The laws relating to delimitation of constituencies are made by the Election Commission of India.
4. No election shall be called in question except by an election petition.
Select the correct answer using the code given below
explanation: The only incorrect statement is 3 — delimitation laws are made by Parliament (and implemented by a Delimitation Commission), not the ECI. Preparing electoral rolls (1), the MCC starting on announcement (2), and challenge only by petition (4) are correct. Hence 3 only.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following constitutional authorities inquires and decides in case of doubts and disputes arising out of election of the President and Vice President of India?
explanation: Under Article 71, all doubts and disputes regarding the election of the President and Vice-President are inquired into and decided by the Supreme Court, whose decision is final.

subject: Polity
stem: Which of the following is not under the powers and functions of the Election Commission of India?
options: Superintendence, direction and control of the preparation of electoral rolls
Conduct of elections to the Parliament and to the Legislature of each State
Conduct of election to the office of the President and Vice President
Appointment of the Regional Commissioners to assist the Election Commission in the performance of the functions conferred on the Commission
explanation: Regional Commissioners are appointed by the President (in consultation with the ECI), not by the ECI itself — so this is not an ECI function. Superintendence of rolls and conduct of parliamentary/assembly and presidential/vice-presidential elections are ECI functions.

subject: Polity
stem: Which of the following statements are correct?
subs: 1. Speaker may permit any Member to address the House in his/her mother tongue, if he/she cannot adequately express in either Hindi or English.
2. Business of the Parliamentary Committees is transacted either in Hindi or in English.
3. The minutes of the Parliamentary Committees are prepared invariably in Hindi or English.
Select the correct answer using the code given below
explanation: All three are correct — a member may, with the Speaker's permission, speak in his mother tongue (1); committee business is conducted in Hindi or English (2); and the minutes are recorded in Hindi or English (3).

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following statements with regard to the Election Commission is not correct?
options: It conducts elections to the office of the Vice President of India.
An Election Commissioner can be removed from his office without the recommendation of the Chief Election Commissioner.
Election Commissioners are appointed by the President of India.
Governor of a State is constitutionally bound to provide support staff to the Election Commission, if required.
explanation: An Election Commissioner can be removed only on the recommendation of the CEC — so removing one "without" that recommendation is not correct (this is a key safeguard of the ECs' independence). The EC conducting the VP election, the President appointing the ECs, and the provision of staff are correct.

subject: Polity
stem: The use of Electors' Photo Identity Cards (EPIC) by the Election Commission of India was started in which one of the following years?
explanation: The Election Commission (under T. N. Seshan) introduced Electors' Photo Identity Cards (EPIC) in 1993 to curb impersonation and electoral fraud.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following statements with regard to the appointment of the Members of the Parliamentary Committees is correct?
options: The Members are appointed or elected on a motion made and adopted or nominated by the Speaker of the Lok Sabha or the Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.
explanation: Members of Parliamentary Committees are either elected (by proportional representation, on a motion) or nominated by the Speaker/Chairman — so option d captures the full position. The other options are too narrow.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following statements about the Solicitor General of India (SGI) is not correct?
options: The SGI is the Government's chief legal advisor.
The SGI is the secondary law officer of the country.
The post of the SGI is a constitutional post.
The SGI is appointed by the Appointments Committee of the Cabinet.
explanation: Intended answer: c) the post of Solicitor General of India is a constitutional post - which is itself the flawed statement, since the SGI is NOT a constitutional post. Note option a is also inaccurate if read strictly: the Attorney General (not the Solicitor General) is the chief legal adviser to the Government of India. So more than one option is flawed.

subject: Polity
stem: Who among the following is the Chairman of the Fourteenth Finance Commission?
explanation: The 14th Finance Commission (which raised the states' share of central taxes to 42%) was chaired by Y. V. Reddy, a former RBI Governor. The others did not chair it.

subject: Polity
stem: Arrange the following Commissions chronologically on the basis of their date of setting
subs: 1. The Second Administrative Reforms Commission
2. The Eleventh Finance Commission
3. Punchhi Commission
4. Sarkaria Commission
Select the correct answer using the code given below
explanation: Order: Sarkaria Commission (1983) → Eleventh Finance Commission (1998) → Second ARC (2005) → Punchhi Commission (2007) = 4, 2, 1, 3.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following criteria got the highest weight for determination of shares of States in the formula given by the 14th Finance Commission?
explanation: In the 14th FC formula, Income distance (the gap of a state's per-capita income from the highest) carried the highest weight (50%), to give more to poorer states. Population, area and others had smaller weights.

subject: Polity
stem: The Fourteenth Finance Commission assigned different weights to the following parameters for distribution of tax proceeds to the States
subs: 1. Income distance
2. Population
3. Demographic changes
4. Area Arrange the aforesaid parameters in descending order in terms of their weights.
explanation: Weights: Income distance 50% → Population (1971) 17.5% → Area 15% → Demographic change (2011) 10%. Descending = Income distance, Population, Area, Demographic changes = 1-2-4-3.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following is the correct sequence of formation of Commissions starting from the earliest?
options: Finance Commission, Planning Commission, Investment Commission, Election Commission
Election Commission, Planning Commission, Finance Commission, Investment Commission
Planning Commission, Election Commission, Finance Commission, Investment Commission
Investment Commission, Finance Commission, Planning Commission, Election Commission
explanation: Order: Election Commission (Jan 1950) → Planning Commission (Mar 1950) → Finance Commission (1951) → Investment Commission (2004) = option b.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following taxes is not included in the Central Pool to be shared with the States according to the recommendations of Finance Commission of India?
explanation: Surcharges and cesses are not part of the divisible pool — the Centre retains them entirely (a reason states complain). Income tax, corporate tax and (Union) excise duties are shareable with the states.

subject: Polity
stem: The audit reports of the Comptroller and Auditor General of India relating to the accounts of the Union shall be submitted to
options: the Speaker of the Lok Sabha
explanation: Under Article 151, the CAG's reports on the Union's accounts go to the President, who has them laid before Parliament (where the Public Accounts Committee examines them).

subject: Polity
stem: G. C. Murmu, who was appointed as the Comptroller and Auditor General of India in August 2020, was the Lieutenant Governor/Administrator of which one of the following Union Territories prior to this appointment?
explanation: G. C. Murmu was the first Lieutenant Governor of the UT of Jammu & Kashmir (after its reorganisation in 2019) before becoming CAG in August 2020.

subject: Polity
stem: Which of the following are the functions of the National Human Rights Commission (NHRC)?
subs: 1. Inquiry at its own initiative on the violation of human rights
2. Inquiry on a petition presented to it by a victim
3. Visit to jails to study the condition of the inmates
4. Undertaking and promoting research in the field of human rights
Select the correct answer using the code given below
explanation: The National Human Rights Commission can inquire suo motu or on petition (1, 2), visit jails to study conditions (3), and promote human-rights research and awareness (4). All four are its functions.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following statements regarding the Human Rights Council is not correct?
options: It is an inter-governmental body within the United Nations system made up of all members of the UN.
It is responsible for the promotion and protection of all human rights around the globe.
It replaced the former United Nations Commission on Human Rights.
It is made up of 47 UN Member States which are elected by the UN General Assembly.
explanation: The UN Human Rights Council has 47 elected member states, not "all members of the UN" — so statement a is false. Its mandate to promote/protect human rights, its replacement of the old Commission (2006), and its 47-member composition are correct.

subject: Polity
stem: In October 2018, India was elected as a member to the United Nations Human Rights Council for a period of
explanation: Members of the UN Human Rights Council serve three-year terms; India was elected for the 2019–2021 term in October 2018.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one among the following is not under the jurisdiction of the Protection of Human Rights Act, 1993?
explanation: The Protection of Human Rights Act, 1993 set up the NHRC, State Human Rights Commissions and Human Rights Courts. The UN Human Rights Council is an international UN body, not a creation of this Act.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following is not an objective of NITI Aayog?
options: It provides a critical direction and strategic input for development process.
It functions as a think tank in providing key elements of policy.
It monitors and evaluates the implementation of the programmes.
It offers a platform for resolution of inter-state conflicts as provider of first and last resort.
explanation: NITI Aayog is essentially a think tank/advisory body — it does not act as the body for resolving inter-state disputes (that role belongs to the Inter-State Council, Article 263). So option d is not its objective. The other roles are correct.

subject: Polity
stem: Which among the following is/are the objective(s) of the NITI Aayog?
subs: 1. Imposing policies on the States/UTs
2. Allocation of funds at National and State levels
3. Design strategies and long-term policies and programme frameworks
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
explanation: NITI Aayog's role is to design strategies and long-term policy frameworks (3). It does not impose policies on states (1, it works cooperatively) and does not allocate funds (2, that ended with the Planning Commission). Hence 3 only.

subject: Polity
stem: According to the National Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) constructed by the NITI Aayog, a household is considered deprived if:
subs: 1. a single member of the household is identified as under-nourished
2. the body mass index of a woman member, between 15 years and 49 years of age, is below 18.5 kg/m�
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
explanation: In the National MPI's nutrition indicator, a household is "deprived" if any single member is undernourished (1), and undernourishment for a woman of 15-49 is taken as a BMI below 18.5 (2). Both criteria are correct.

subject: Polity
stem: Which of the following sets of States is at the bottom of the Composite SDG India Index, 2020-2021 as per the NITI Aayog?
explanation: In the SDG India Index 2020-21, the lowest-performing States were Bihar, Jharkhand and Assam (with Kerala on top) — matching option a.

subject: Polity
stem: As per the Cabinet Resolution of 1st January, 2015, the NITI Aayog consists of:
subs: 1. Prime Minister of India
2. Governing Council comprising of Chief Minister of all the States and Lieutenant Governors of Union Territories
3. Rural Development Minister
4. Chief Executive officer to be appointed by the Prime Minister
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
explanation: NITI Aayog has the PM as Chairperson (1), a Governing Council of all Chief Ministers and UT Lt. Governors (2), and a CEO appointed by the PM (4). The Rural Development Minister (3) is not specifically named in its structure. Hence 1, 2 and 4.

subject: Polity
stem: SDG India Index, developed by NITI Aayog, includes 17 SDGs for each State. Which one of the following is not included in that index?
explanation: SDG 8 is "Decent Work and Economic Growth", not "Reduced unemployment" — so the latter is not an SDG title. Zero Hunger (SDG 2), Life Below Water (SDG 14) and Responsible Consumption and Production (SDG 12) are genuine SDGs.

subject: Polity
stem: Which of the following statements about the NITI Aayog is/are correct?
subs: 1. Prime Minister of India is the Chairperson of its Governing Council.
2. Lieutenant Governor of Ladakh is a member of its Governing Council.
Select the answer using the code given below
explanation: The PM chairs the Governing Council (1), and the Lt. Governors of UTs (including Ladakh) are members of the Governing Council (2). Both correct.

subject: Polity
stem: Consider the following statements about Central Vigilance Commission (CVC)
subs: 1. Central Vigilance Commissioner is appointed by the President of India.
2. The Committee to recommend the appointment of Central Vigilance Commissioner has the Minister of Home Affairs as one of the members.

3. In certain circumstances the Leader of the single largest group in opposition in Lok Sabha can be on the Committee to recommend the appointment of Central Vigilance Commissioner. How many of the above statements is/are correct?
explanation: All three are correct — the CVC is appointed by the President (1) on the recommendation of a committee comprising the PM, the Home Minister (2) and the Leader of Opposition in the Lok Sabha; where there is no recognised LoP, the leader of the single largest opposition group sits on it (3). So 3.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following is not correct about Administrative Tribunals?
options: The Parliament may by law constitute Administrative Tribunals both at the Union and State levels.
Tribunals may look into disputes and complaints with respect to recruitment and conditions of service of persons appointed to public services.
Tribunals established by a law of the Parliament can exclude the jurisdiction of all Courts to allow for special leave to appeal.
The law establishing the Tribunals may provide for procedures including rules of evidence to be followed.
explanation: Tribunals cannot exclude the Supreme Court's jurisdiction under Article 136 (special leave) — and after L. Chandra Kumar (1997), not the High Courts' under Articles 226/227 either. So statement c is false. The power to set up tribunals (Articles 323A/323B), their service-matter jurisdiction, and their own procedures are correct.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following statements with regard to the National Commission for Scheduled Tribes is not correct?
options: The Union and every State Government shall consult the Commission on all major policy matters affecting Scheduled Tribes.
All the reports of the Commission and its recommendations shall be laid only before Lok Sabha.
The Commission, while investigating any matter, has all the powers of a Civil Court.
The Commission has the power to regulate its own procedures.
explanation: The NCST's reports are laid before both Houses of Parliament (and the relevant State Legislature), not only the Lok Sabha — so statement b is false. Consultation on ST policy, civil-court powers during inquiry, and regulating its own procedure are correct.

subject: Polity
stem: The National Commission for Women was created by
options: an amendment in the Constitution of India
a decision of the Union Cabinet
an Act passed by the Parliament
an order of the President of India
explanation: The National Commission for Women is a statutory body, set up under the National Commission for Women Act, 1990. It is not a constitutional body (unlike the NCSC/NCST).

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following Commissions is related to Article 338A?
options: The National Commission for Scheduled Castes
The National Commission for Scheduled Tribes
The National Commission for Backward Classes
explanation: Article 338A provides for the National Commission for Scheduled Tribes (created by the 89th Amendment, 2003, splitting the earlier combined commission). Article 338 is for the NCSC; 338B (102nd Amendment) for the NCBC.

subject: Polity
stem: Which of the following statements about National Commission for Women are correct?
subs: 1. It is a statutory body.
2. It has the mandate to review the constitutional and legal safeguards for women.
3. It has the mandate to advise the Government on all policy matters affecting women.
Select the answer using the code given below
explanation: The NCW is a statutory body (1, under the 1990 Act), it reviews the constitutional/legal safeguards for women (2), and it advises the government on policy matters affecting women (3). All three are correct.

subject: Polity
subtopic: Electoral system & Model Code of Conduct
stem: In the elections to the provincial legislatures in 1937 in British India
subs: 1. only about 10 to 12 percent of the population had the right to vote
2. the untouchables had no right to vote
3. the Congress won an absolute majority infive out of eleven provinces
4. the Muslim League won more than 80 percent of the seats reserved for Muslims
Select the correct answer using the code given below
explanation: The franchise was narrow (~10-12%) (1) and the Congress swept five of eleven provinces (3). Statement 2 is wrong (the depressed classes did vote, with reserved seats) and statement 4 is wrong — the Muslim League fared poorly, winning only a small share of Muslim seats. Hence 1 and 3 only.

subject: Polity
subtopic: Electoral system & Model Code of Conduct
stem: The mobile app cVIGIL is helpful in
options: conducting free and fair e-tendering process in government of ces
ghting against corruption in public services
removing garbage from the municipal areas
reporting violation of model code of conduct in election-bound States
explanation: cVIGIL is an Election Commission app that lets citizens report Model Code of Conduct violations (with photo/video and auto-location) during elections, for quick action.

subject: Polity
subtopic: Electoral system & Model Code of Conduct
stem: In how many phases was the general election, 2019 conducted in India?
explanation: The 2019 Lok Sabha general election was held in seven phases (April–May 2019) across the country.

subject: Polity
subtopic: Electoral system & Model Code of Conduct
stem: Overseas Indians can exercise franchise in an election to the Lok Sabha under which of the following conditions?
subs: 1. They must be citizens of India.
2. Their names must figure in the electoral roll.
3. They must be present in India to vote.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
explanation: An NRI voter must be an Indian citizen (1), be registered on the electoral roll of his/her constituency (2), and currently must be physically present to vote (3) (proxy/postal voting for NRIs has not yet been operationalised). Hence all three.

subject: Polity
subtopic: Electoral system & Model Code of Conduct
stem: Which of the following statements regarding the electoral rolls is/are correct?
subs: 1. There is one general electoral roll for every territorial constituency.
2. No person is ineligible for inclusion in the electoral roll on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex, or any of them.
3. A citizen of India, not less than 18 years of age, can cast his/her vote unless disqualified under a law of an appropriate legislature.
Select the correct answer using the code given below
explanation: Articles 325 and 326 guarantee one general electoral roll per constituency (1), no exclusion on religion/race/caste/sex (1 roll, not separate communal rolls) (2), and the right to vote for any citizen aged 18+ unless disqualified (3). All three are correct.

subject: Polity
subtopic: Electoral system & Model Code of Conduct
stem: In the election held in 1937, in which two provinces was the Indian National Congress not able to emerge as the single largest party?
explanation: The Congress dominated most provinces but not Punjab (where the Unionist Party won) or Sind. It was the largest party in Madras and Assam. Hence Punjab and Sind.

subject: Polity
stem: The First Delimitation Commission in India was constituted in
explanation: The first Delimitation Commission was set up in 1952 (under the Delimitation Commission Act, 1952) to draw constituency boundaries for the first general elections.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following statements about the All India Depressed Classes Association is not correct?
options: The All India Depressed Classes Association was formed in Nagpur with M. C. Rajah as its first elected President.
The All India Depressed Classes Association was not attended by Dr. B. R. Ambedkar in 1926.
Ambedkar resigned from the All India Depressed Classes Association and formed his own All India Depressed Classes Congress in 1930.
The All India Depressed Classes Association favoured Ambedkar's demand for separate electorate for the depressed classes.
explanation: M. C. Rajah's Association favoured joint electorates (leading to the Rajah-Moonje Pact) and thus opposed Ambedkar's separate-electorate demand — so statement d is false. The rest (Nagpur founding, Ambedkar's 1926 absence, his 1930 breakaway) are correct.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following statements about the Justice Party is not correct?
options: It clamoured for the same kind of separate communal representation for the Non-Brahmins as had been granted to the Muslims by the Morley-Minto Reforms.
It was patronized mainly by richer landowning and urban middle class Non-Brahmins.
It succeeded in getting the provision for 28 reserved seats for the Non-Brahmins in the Montagu-Chelmsford Reforms.
It supported the call given by the Congress to boycott elections in 1920.
explanation: The Justice Party (Madras) contested the 1920 elections and took office, opposing the Congress boycott — so statement d is false. Its communal-representation demand, its landed/middle-class base, and the 28 reserved seats are correct.

subject: Polity
stem: According to the Election Commission of India, in order to be recognized as a National Party, a political party must be treated as a recognized political party in how many States?
explanation: One way to qualify as a National Party is to be recognised as a State party in at least four States (there are also vote-share and seat-based criteria). Hence four States.

subject: Polity
stem: What was the Dutt-Bradley thesis?
options: The Working Committee of the Indian National Congress decided that Congress should play a crucial role in realising the independence of India
The Socialist party decided to play foremost part in anti-imperialist struggle
Revolutionary socialist Batukeshwar Dutt put forth a ten-point plan to work for the success of anti-imperialist front
It was a Communist party document, according to which the National Congress could play a great part and a foremost part in realising the anti-imperialist peoples front
explanation: The Dutt-Bradley thesis (1936) was a Communist Party document (R. P. Dutt and Ben Bradley) arguing that the Congress could be the chief vehicle of a broad anti-imperialist people's front.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one among the following was demanded by the All India Depressed Classes Leaders Conference at Bombay in 1931?
explanation: Reflecting Ambedkar's position, the conference demanded separate electorates for the untouchables — the issue that led to the Communal Award and then the Poona Pact.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following is not a classified category of political parties as outlined by the Election Commission of India?
explanation: The ECI classifies parties as National, State (recognised) and Registered Unrecognised. "Regional Parties" is a loose, informal term — not an official ECI category.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following is not a political method of the moderates in the National Movement?
explanation: The Moderates worked within the system — constitutional agitation, gradual progress and educating public opinion ("prayer, petition, protest"). They did not aim to overthrow British rule. Hence a.

subject: Polity
stem: In which one of the following years was the Poona Sarvajanik Sabha established?
explanation: The Poona Sarvajanik Sabha was founded in 1870 (by Ranade and others) as a body to mediate between the government and the people — an early nationalist association.

subject: Polity
stem: Who among the following political leaders suggested the dissolution of the Indian National Congress as a political organization and its replacement by a Lok Sevak Sangh?
explanation: In his last note (Jan 1948), Gandhi proposed that the Congress — having won freedom — be disbanded as a party and reorganised into a Lok Sevak Sangh (a body of social workers).

subject: Polity
stem: Consider the following statements about the early phase of the National Movement in India:
subs: 1. Ferozeshah Mehta, Badruddin Tyabji and others formed the Bombay Presidency Association.
2. Surendranath Banerjee and his group planned an Indian National Conference at Calcutta.

3. Allan Octavian Hume decided to create an all-India body as a rival to the above organisations to give vent to the grievances of the poor and marginalised Indians. How many of the above statements is/are correct?
explanation: Only two statements are correct. The statement about A.O. Hume and the formation of the INC is inaccurate in its framing - the Congress emerged as an all-India political platform, not simply as a rival to earlier associations. Hence the answer is 2.

subject: Polity
stem: The distribution of charkhas on a wide scale among the people was decided by
options: Mahatma Gandhi at the start of Dandi March
explanation: As part of the Non-Cooperation constructive programme, the AICC in 1921 decided to popularise the charkha and khadi widely — central to Gandhi's self-reliance and swadeshi programme.

subject: Polity
stem: Who among the following is the founder of the Congress Socialist Party?
explanation: The Congress Socialist Party (1934) was founded by socialists within the Congress, with Acharya Narendra Dev as its first president (and Jayaprakash Narayan as general secretary).

subject: Polity
stem: Chronologically arrange the formation of the following political associations, beginning from the earliest
subs: 1. Poona Sarvajanik Sabha
2. Indian Association
3. Madras Mahajan Sabha
4. Bombay Presidency Association
Select the answer using the code given below
explanation: Chronology: Poona Sarvajanik Sabha (1870) → Indian Association (1876) → Madras Mahajan Sabha (1884) → Bombay Presidency Association (1885) = 1, 2, 3, 4.

subject: Polity
stem: Under Article 352 of the Constitution of India, an emergency can be declared if security of any part of India is threatened by
subs: 1. war
2. external aggression
3. armed rebellion
4. internal disturbance
Select the correct answer using the code given below
explanation: A National Emergency (Article 352) can be proclaimed on grounds of war, external aggression or armed rebellion (1, 2, 3). "Internal disturbance" (4) was the original third ground but was replaced by "armed rebellion" by the 44th Amendment (1978). Hence 1, 2 and 3.

subject: Polity
stem: The Fundamental Rights guaranteed in the Constitution of India can be suspended only by
options: an Act passed by the Parliament
an amendment to the Constitution of India
the judicial decisions of the Supreme Court
explanation: Fundamental Rights can be suspended only during a National Emergency (Article 352) — Article 358 automatically suspends Article 19 (external emergency), and Article 359 lets the President suspend the enforcement of other rights (except Articles 20 and 21).

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following statements about emergency provisions under the Constitution of India is not correct?
options: The powers of the Union Executive extend to giving directions to the States concerning the exercise of their powers.
The Union Executive can issue a provision relating to reduction of salaries of employees of the State Governments.
Governors have no emergency powers like the President of India.
If the Governor of a State is satis ed that a situation has arisen whereby thefinancial stability or credit of the State is threatened, he may declare financial emergency in the State.
explanation: A financial emergency (Article 360) is declared by the President (for the whole country), not by a Governor for a State — so statement d is false. The Union directing States, reducing salaries during a financial emergency, and Governors lacking emergency powers are correct.

subject: Polity
stem: When a Proclamation of Emergency is in operation, the right to move a Court for the enforcement of all Fundamental Rights remains suspended, except
explanation: After the 44th Amendment, even during an emergency the enforcement of Articles 20 (protection in respect of conviction) and 21 (life and personal liberty) cannot be suspended. So these two are the exception.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following amendments in the Constitution of India made a Proclamation of Emergency immune from judicial review?
explanation: [Defective question] The correct answer is the 38th Amendment, which made the Proclamation of Emergency immune from judicial review. The printed options do not include the 38th Amendment.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following is not a circumstance for proclamation of Emergency by the President of India under Article 352 of the Constitution of India?
explanation: "Internal disturbance" is no longer a ground — the 44th Amendment replaced it with "armed rebellion." So War, external aggression and armed rebellion are the grounds; internal disturbance is not.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following is not mentioned as a form of Emergency in the Constitution of India?
options: State Emergency in terms of Proclamation of President's Rule in a State
explanation: The Constitution provides for three emergencies — National (352), State/President's Rule (356) and Financial (360). A "Health Emergency" is not a constitutional category.

subject: Polity
stem: Which among the following Fundamental Rights is not suspended when an Emergency is declared?
options: Protection in respect of conviction for offences
Right to move freely throughout the territory of India
explanation: Article 20 (protection in respect of conviction) — along with Article 21 — cannot be suspended even during an emergency (44th Amendment). The right to move freely (Article 19) and others can be suspended, so option a is the one not suspended.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following Articles was defended by Dr. B. R. Ambedkar on the plea that it would be used as a matter of last resort?
explanation: Ambedkar defended Article 356 (President's Rule in a State), hoping it would remain a "dead letter" and be used only as a last resort. (In practice it has been used/misused many times.)

subject: Polity
stem: Constitutional safeguards available to Civil Servants are ensured by
explanation: Article 311 gives civil servants protection against arbitrary dismissal, removal or reduction in rank (e.g. the right to an inquiry and a hearing). Article 310 is the "doctrine of pleasure," 312 is on All India Services, 317 on removal of a PSC member.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following statements about the All India Services is correct?
options: The All India Services may be created by an Act of the Parliament.
The endorsement of the Rajya Sabha is not essential for the creation of the All India Services.
The rules of recruitment to the All India Services are determined by the UPSC.
The conditions of service to the All India Services may be altered by the UPSC.
explanation: Under Article 312, Parliament can create new All India Services — but only after the Rajya Sabha passes a resolution by a two-thirds majority. So statement a is correct, while b (RS endorsement IS essential) and c/d (the UPSC does not frame the rules/conditions) are wrong.

subject: Polity
stem: Mission Satyanishtha, a programme on ethics in public governance, was launched recently by the
explanation: Mission Satyanishtha was launched by the Indian Railways to sensitise its employees to ethics and integrity in public service.

subject: Polity
stem: Who among the following is the Chairman of the Economic Advisory Council to the Prime Minister (EAC-PM)?
explanation: At the time of the paper, Bibek Debroy chaired the EAC-PM (an independent body advising the PM on economic matters). The others were members/associated economists.

subject: Polity
stem: National Disaster Management Authority is headed by
explanation: The NDMA, the apex disaster-management body (under the DM Act 2005), is chaired by the Prime Minister. (It functions under the Ministry of Home Affairs.)

subject: Polity
stem: Which of the following are the components of the Indian Legal System?
subs: 1. Basic values and principles enshrined in the Constitution
2. Code Rights and obligations conferred by ordinary statutes
3. Organizational set-up to enforce rights and obligations within the constitutional norms
4. Legal and judicial persons
Select the answer using the code given below
